item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Love the energy. I, personally, am working on a cute-but-terrifying scp, and just really enjoyed reading this. 5104-1 is adorable, and the chiropractor is hilariously unaware of the situation he's in.

The nonchalant "eh, giant bone snake hasn't killed anyone, let's give it what it wants" from the foundation staff if also great. I love the idea that they're so used to world-ending horrors that when something just wants its spine set, they're willing to just accept it and throw a dude in there with an entirely unknown anomaly.

Since the wiki doesn't have red shapes, you get 1 little cross with a number 1 after it, and a spot on my list of favorite scps.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Shouldn't the several hundred additional entities receive SCP designations? Shouldn't the Foundation perhaps take an interest in establishing communication with this species, if only in order to negotiate a less Veil-endangering place for them to portal in next time?

The Foundation presented in this scip seems amateurish and sloppy. Even compliant sentient entities are nontrivial to contain. It doesn't help that we're just offhandedly told that the second round of containment was successful, easy peasy, as if the evaluator is carefree and disinterested. There isn't even a cliché note about administering amnestics to witnesses.

I guess I'm in the minority, given that the article has 150+ upvotes. It's not all bad. I like the anomaly itself. I think its use of Yelp is cute, in a good way. But even the more cringe-inducing articles of the LOLFoundation era don't present this cavalier an institution.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought this was fantastic. I don't even see it as breaking immersion to think that some of the on-site personnel would utilize how services, he has Level 1 clearance now so it's not or of the realm of possibility.

Hell, they do timey-wimey stuff, have thaumaturges from multiple disciplines, affective esoteric eschatological practitioners from many faiths, Sarkic body horror specialists, and other things almost too numerous to list…. And CHIROPRACTICS is where the line is drawn? 🤔

one green plus shape from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've made some changes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your concerns have been rectified. I truly apologize for being such a fake Philadelphia fan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Geography error: "Berwyn, Philadelphia" presumably should be "Berwyn, Pennsylvania." And Berwyn is a suburban neighborhood, not a municipality that would have its own police department. The municipality that it's in (which has a police department) is Easttown Township, Pennsylvania.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So a few… issues I have with this related to it's setting:
- Philadelphia is a city not a state
- Berwyn isn't even in Philadelphia county it's in Chester county.
- It's not technically a town, it's an area designated by the census bureau.
- It doesn't have a mayor because of this
- Someone who lived in the area designated as Berwyn would likely say they lived in Easttown.

Your easy fix here is to replace Philadelphia with Illinois.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The author is still here, they are J Dune.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is cute and funny.

Oh no what happened to the author?!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm aware of the specifics, but my thoughts remain the same.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Based on the response the author gave to my comments regarding chiropractic, he was under the assumption that a chiropractor IS a massage therapist.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're aware the chiropractor is only there because it's an effective way to contain the anomaly? The medical benefits or lack thereof aren't relevant here, because the chiropractor is only there since it keeps the anomaly satisfied.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yes I have read comedic articles. I'm not sure where the comedy in that part I referenced was.
No, the foundation specifically reprimands researchers that behave this way. Lightening the mood is one thing, this is another.

As I said in my comment (proving you did not actually read the whole thing), chiropractic is NOT a massage. It is a completely different thing.
The foundation is not a real thing, what they practice are things that, within the lore, are considered real and have their own "science". Exorcisms are not really pseudoscience since no one really claims them to have scientific value, only religious.
If you want me to buy the foundation using chiropractors, you're better off making it it's own SCP.

At the end of the day, I'm aware that there is no set "canon" in a strict sense and that nothing you've written breaks any real "rules". My comment was simply my subjective thoughts on what I disliked about it, nothing more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thought this was Long Horse at first. Pretty cute. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: i give this scp article one green plus shape

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Despite my issues with chiropractic in general, I gotta say that this is a great article, and it honestly wouldn't have worked at all if the POI was a massage therapist or whatever.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. Have you read any comedic article, like, ever? Why is it unreasonable to assume someone would want to break the ice and try to lighten the mood in an interview with a civilian? Researchers are people too.

2. The entity likes to have its spine massaged. I don't see why this is a huge deal. Are you also surprised that the Foundation performs exorcisms and other pseudoscientific practices? Do you know where you are?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's okay in theory, but there's 2 major things that turned me off to it.
1: the beginning of the interview is the doctor being like "ha nah I'm fucking with you." Since when the hell are the doctors at the foundation 12 year olds who act like this?
2: Really? The foundation is using a chiropractor? Chiropractic is a pseudo-science that is not respected by any actual doctors and is honestly only really taken seriously in some parts of the USA. (Note: some people mistake chiropractics for a type of massage therapy. It is not. Massage therapy is real, chiropractics has more religious origins by a guy who performed a seance and claimed a ghost taught him the secrets to healing all ailments) Is the foundation also subscribing to other bullshit like homeopathy and accupuncture?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Understandable. I meant for it to be tongue-in-cheek dumb, but I get we’re you’re coming from. I’ll see what I can do about it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The dialogue here is a bit cheesy for my tastes. This line in particular stuck out to me:

I'm a chiropractor! I don't do that pagan shit, if that's what you're trying to say!

Otherwise, I like the idea, and the dialogue is the only thing keeping me from upvoting. Novote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: They liked it! I showed it to them in the draft stages.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Has M8_Ball had any reaction to the article?
Vivid characterization, realistic dialogue, and a touch of surreal cuteness. What more could an SCP article need?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I saw this before you reworked the draft.

I was honored when you messaged me to review the rewritten version. The end result is something you may be proud of.

+1, good luck in the future :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
sometimes a good SCP is just a weird bone snake with a back problem

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If I hadn't been paying attention to context clues, that might've been a fun twist.

What a beautiful line. A serious contender for Top 10 single lines in any SCP I've seen.

Very funny, very cute. Fun escalation at the end. Just solid work all around.
this is a good scp i like this one a lot!
This hits a very specific note for me, I like terrifying things that are cute. Very nice work.

heard good

have upvote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 5 red shapes. Good shit as a draft, good shit now.
I reviewed this as a draft, and absolutely loved it. Awesome to see it on the main site! It's a fun concept and is very funny. Good job! +100000000000000

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Quite a few people looked this over. Thanks to Hexick, Diana, Mew, sirslash, Cybersqyd, Jackal, Void, and Popsioak for being cool.

ill localize the images if it survives

bone dragon was made by M8_Ball. Their instagram is in the licensing box. Check them out, they do great work.
